title: Leucas biflora
intro: Leucas biflora, also known as bitter button plant, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Lamiaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leucas biflora include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Leucas biflora is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, and it is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
taxonomy: Leucas biflora is a species of plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint or deadnettle family. This plant belongs to the genus Leucas, which consists of various species of herbs and shrubs. The genus Leucas is further classified under the subfamily Lamioideae and the order Lamiales. Leucas biflora is native to tropical regions and is known for its medicinal properties. The classification of Leucas biflora is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
uses: Leucas biflora is a plant that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin infections. properties: Leucas biflora is known for its diverse medicinal properties. preparations: Leucas biflora is known to be used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations that are believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
